## Basic Ideas and Concepts

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

::: {.fragment fragment-index=1}
- **Define networks in JAX:** dynamics, coupling, graph, noise, inputs
:::
<div style="height: 2.2em;"></div>

::: {.fragment fragment-index=2}
- **Separation of computation from data:** `prepare` returns a pure function + state
:::

::: {.fragment fragment-index=3}
- **Incremental complexity:** \n compose simple pieces: `loss(observation(model(state)))`
:::
<div style="height: 0.1em;"></div>

::: {.fragment fragment-index=4}
- **Build once, run anywhere:** CPU, GPU, TPU without code changes
:::

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}

::: {.fragment fragment-index=1}
```{python}
#| eval: false
#| echo: true
network = Network(
    dynamics=ReducedWongWang(w=0.7),
    coupling=DelayedLinearCoupling(G=0.5),
    graph=DenseDelayGraph(weights, delays),
    noise=AdditiveNoise(sigma=0.01),
    external_input={"stimulus": PulseTrainInput()},
)
```
:::
<div style="height: 0.1em;"></div>

::: {.fragment fragment-index=2}
```{python}
#| eval: false
#| echo: true
model, state = prepare(
    network, Heun(), t1=1000.0, dt=1.0
    )
result = model(state) # same as solve(network, ...)
```
:::
<div style="height: 0.1em;"></div>

::: {.fragment fragment-index=3}

![](figures/loss_def.png){width="70%"}
<!-- 
```{python}
#| eval: false
#| echo: true
def observe(state):
    return compute_fc(model(state))

def loss(state):
    return rmse(observe(state), fc_emp)
```
 -->
:::
<!-- <div style="height: 0.1em;"></div> -->
::: {.fragment fragment-index=4}
![](figures/jax_gpu_cpu.png){width="70%"}

<!-- ```{python}
#| eval: false
#| echo: true
# Same code, any backend
jax.devices("cpu")  # or "gpu", "tpu"

# Automatic vectorization & parallelization
batched = jax.vmap(model)(states)
distributed = jax.pmap(model)(states)
``` -->
:::

:::
::::

## Performance Compare to TVB

::: {.fragment}
![](figures/tvboptim_perf.png){width="70%"}
:::

::: {.fragment}
$\rightarrow$ robustly >10x faster on CPU
:::

## Easy Explorations

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="75%"}

::: {.fragment}
Define a Parameter Space:

<div style="height: 0.5em;"></div>

```{python}
#| eval: false
#| echo: true
#| code-line-numbers: "1|3-4|5-6|8-9"
model, state = prepare(network, Heun(), t1=1000, dt=1.0)

state.external.stimulus.amplitude = DataAxis([0.5, 1.0, 2.0, 5.0], group='stim')
state.external.stimulus.delay     = DataAxis([10., 20., 50., 80.], group='stim')
state.coupling.G                  = GridAxis(low=0.01, high=0.5, 8)
state.noise.sigma                 = LogGridAxis(low=1e-4, high=1e-1, 4)

space = Space(state, mode='product')
# 4 stimuli x 8 G x 4 sigma = 128 simulations
```
:::

:::

::: {.column width="25%"}

::: {.fragment}
![](figures/param_space.png){width="100%"}
:::

:::
:::: 

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="75%"}

::: {.fragment}

<br><br>

Execute computations over a Space:

<div style="height: 0.5em;"></div>

```{python}
#| eval: false
#| echo: true
#| code-line-numbers: "1|2"

executor = ParallelExecution(loss, space, n_vmap=len(space), n_pmap=1)
losses = executor.run()
```
:::

:::

::: {.column width="25%"}

::: {.fragment}
![](figures/param_space_loss.png){width="100%"}
:::

:::
:::: 

## JAX: Zero-Effort GPU Acceleration

:::{.fragment}
```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
#| code-line-numbers: "1-2|4-5|7-10"
import jax
import jax.numpy as jnp

# JAX automatically detects available GPUs
print(jax.devices())

# Code is identical whether on CPU or GPU
x = jnp.ones((5000, 5000))  # allocated on GPU if available
result = x @ x                # runs on GPU if available
print(f"Computed on: {result.devices()}")
```
:::

:::{.r-stack}
:::{.fragment}
![](figures/gpu_perf.png){width="100%"}
:::

:::{.fragment}
[10x]{.absolute top="380" left="90" width="60" height="50" style="border: 4px solid red; border-radius: 4px; font-weight: bold; font-size: 0.6em; display: flex; align-items: top; justify-content: center;"}
:::

:::{.fragment}
[1000x]{.absolute top="540" left="950" width="60" height="70" style="border: 4px solid red; border-radius: 4px; font-weight: bold; font-size: 0.6em; display: flex; align-items: top; justify-content: center;"}
:::
:::


## The Power of Reverse-Mode Automatic Differentiation (AD)

::: {.incremental}
- Parameter spaces explode combinatorially $\rightarrow$ grid search becomes infeasible at scale
- Reverse-mode AD computes gradients w.r.t. **all** parameters in a single pass $\rightarrow$ $\mathcal{O}(1)$
:::

::: {.fragment}

![](figures/ad_perf.png){width="70%"}

:::

## High-Dimensional Parameter Optimization

<div style="height: 0.5em;"></div>

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="75%"}

::: {.fragment}
Mark parameters for optimization:
```{python}
#| eval: false
#| echo: true
#| code-line-numbers: "1|2"
state.dynamics.w = Parameter(state.dynamics.w)
state.coupling.G = BoundedParameter(state.coupling.G, low=0.0, high=jnp.inf)
```
:::

::: {.fragment}
Define a loss and optimize:
```{python}
#| eval: false
#| echo: true
#| code-line-numbers: "1|2"
opt = OptaxOptimizer(loss, optax.adam(learning_rate=1e-3))
optimized_state, opt_data = opt.run(state, max_steps=500)
```
:::

:::
::: {.column width="25%"}
::: {.fragment}
![](figures/opt_trace.png){width="70%"}
:::
:::
::::

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="75%"}

<br><br>

::: {.fragment}
Refine with heterogeneous parameters:
```{python}
#| eval: false
#| echo: true
#| code-line-numbers: "1|2|"
optimized_state.dynamics.w.shape = (n_nodes,)
final_state, opt_data = opt.run(optimized_state, max_steps=500)
```
:::

:::

::: {.column width="25%"}

<br><br>

::: {.fragment}
![](figures/heterogeneous_parameter.png){width="70%"}
:::
:::
::::
## Going Beyond: Hybrid Brain Network Models

* UDEs
* Bayesian Inference (SBI vs. HMC)
